calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Plaintiff Discover Bank's unopposed Motion for Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR.

On January 22, 2026, Plaintiff presented for filing a "request to Vacate MSJ Set for 1/26/26." Consistent with Plaintiff's request, the instant motion is ordered off calendar. Plaintiff and counsel, however, are admonished going forward to comply with LRSF 8.2(C).

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. ARIANNA COOK-THAJUDEEN, Esq. of Legal Assistance to the Elderly (Tel: 415-538-3333) for defendant Grace T Casanas;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GRACE CASANAS's Motion To Vacate Default Judgement.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "motion to vacate default and default judgment (CCP 473)" is granted. Defendant declares that she has limited English and financial skills and thought she was diminishing her debts by paying a debt management firm $493.14 a month. Thus, the 65-year-old shows "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect" well within CCP 473(b)'s six-month window. (Id.) Further, the California courts' policy is to adjudicate cases on their merits.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
